stories.headline: Bitcoin cracks $77K as 3% slide erases a full week of ETF-fueled gains
stories.tldr: BTC slid nearly 3% to break below $77,000, wiping out the seven-day rally that briefly tagged the $80K wall.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A clean failure at $80K with concentrated sells flips the dominant institutional narrative back to range-bound chop and squeezes leveraged longs.
the_details: Bitcoin breached $77,000 on April 28 after stalling at the $80K resistance for the prior week.
BTC dominance still sits near 60%, and altcoin season index lingers at 33-38/100.
Spot ETF inflows had been the main bid; the rejection caps a nine-day positive flow streak.

stories.headline: Atkins teases SEC innovation exemptions; Strategic Bitcoin Reserve unveil 'in coming weeks'
stories.tldr: At Bitcoin 2026 in Vegas, SEC Chair Paul Atkins flagged imminent on-chain tokenization sandboxes while the White House previewed a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve rollout.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A formal exemption regime plus a sovereign BTC stash converts US policy from talking points to live market structure within weeks.
the_details: Atkins: SEC will allow companies to test on-chain tokenization and securitization tools in a regulated environment.
Patrick Witt, exec director of the White House Digital Assets Council, said a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve announcement is imminent.
Heads of SEC, CFTC and the White House all spoke at Bitcoin 2026, which kicked off April 27 in Las Vegas.

stories.headline: Four central banks set rates this week - Fed, BoJ, ECB and BoE all loaded into one tape
stories.tldr: The BoJ, Fed, ECB and Bank of England all deliver policy decisions in the same week, stacking macro risk onto a fragile crypto bid.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A synchronized policy week means correlated dollar moves, and BTC's near-perfect inverse correlation with DXY is at its most extreme in nearly four years.
the_details: BoJ, Federal Reserve, ECB, and Bank of England all set rate policy during the week of April 27.
Robinhood and Galaxy Digital also report earnings this week, adding crypto-equity gamma.
CoinDesk flagged BTC vs. dollar opposition as the strongest in nearly 4 years.

stories.headline: Solana spot ETF inflows collapse 92% as SOL grinds at $86 into Firedancer
stories.tldr: Spot SOL ETF inflows fell from $419M in November 2025 to just $34M in April 2026, even as Firedancer targets a 1M TPS upgrade.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Institutional appetite for L1 alts is evaporating just as Solana ships its biggest performance leap, decoupling tech delivery from capital flows.
the_details: SOL trades near $86.47 with a $49.8B market cap, currently the seventh-largest crypto asset.
Firedancer client targets ~1M TPS, up from the current ~1,300 TPS baseline.
Live Alpenglow upgrade already cut transaction finality to under 200ms.

stories.headline: Jupiter unlocks 1.54% of JUP supply today, dumping $9.67M of fresh float on the market
stories.tldr: On April 28, Jupiter's scheduled cliff releases 1.54% of circulating JUP supply worth roughly $9.67 million into the float.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Concentrated unlocks on Solana's flagship DEX aggregator regularly compress price for 24-72h and seed shorts ahead of distribution.
the_details: Unlock size: 1.54% of circulating JUP supply, valued at $9.67M at current prices.
Jupiter is Solana's largest DEX aggregator by volume and a key altcoin liquidity venue.
Token unlocks of this scale historically pressure JUP spot through the next session.
